question: Who went camping in the story?
ground_truth_answer: Luis, Maya, and Dad went camping.
document:
# 2nd Grade Text
# The Camping Trip
Luis and Maya were going camping with Dad. They were packing a tent, a bag, and food. Maya was singing as she put on a red cap. Luis was humming as he looked at a map.
When they got to the hill, Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What did Luis help with at the camp?
ground_truth_answer: Luis helped hold the tent up.

question: What did Min and Kim dig in?
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## Min, Kim, and the Big Dig
Min has a kit.
Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

question: What helps fish swim fast in a pond?
ground_truth_answer: Fish swim fast by swishing their fins.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## Fish in the Pond
Fish can swim fast in a pond. A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash. Some fish can rush to get food. A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.
Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

question: What can a fish snap up to eat?
ground_truth_answer: A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.

Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

question: Why do you think fish need soft bodies?
ground_truth_answer: Soft bodies help fish swim easily in water.

question: What was Ming digging for in the sand?
ground_truth_answer: Ming was digging to find a shell.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## The Missing Ring
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

question: Who did Ming show the ring to?
ground_truth_answer: Ming showed the ring to Dad.

question: Who went camping with Luis and Maya?

document:
# 2nd Grade Text
# The Camping Trip
Luis and Maya were going camping with Dad. They were packing a tent, a bag, and food. Maya was singing as she put on a red cap. Luis was humming as he looked at a map.
When they got to the hill, Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What was Maya wearing while packing?
ground_truth_answer: Maya was wearing a red cap.

As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What is something you enjoy doing at night under the stars?
ground_truth_answer: I enjoy looking at the stars or having a campfire.

# 1st Grade Text
## Min, Kim, and the Big Dig
Min has a kit.
Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

# 1st Grade Text
## Fish in the Pond
Fish can swim fast in a pond. A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash. Some fish can rush to get food. A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.
Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

ground_truth_answer: Fish use their fins to swim.

question: What was Ming hoping to find?

document:
# 1st Grade Text
## The Missing Ring
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

question: What did Ming find while digging?
ground_truth_answer: Ming found a ring.

question: What was Chuck's job?
document:
Once upon a time, at a sunny ranch, there was a chap named Chuck.  

Chuck had a job to chop the wood for lunch.  

His chum, Chip, came to help.  

They sat on the bench to chat and made a plan.  

First, they went to check the big log.  

They used an ax to chop inch by inch.  

Each chop made a loud crunch. 
 
A small chick ran to check the noise. 
 
"It is much work," said Chuck, wiping his chin.  

Chip had a hunch they needed more help.  

They pulled a bunch of logs to the side.  

As they worked, they took a sip to quench thirst.  

At last, they clinched the task.  

They were glad to have such a good friend.

question: What is the starting point of the race?
ground_truth_answer: The red gate
document:
Ben and Meg are at the park.  
They have a race to plan.  

“This race will be nice!” says Meg.  
Ben grins and sets a fast pace.
  
The race will start at the red gate.  
The first one to the big tree will win. 
 
They hope to win a fun prize.  
Ben and Meg trace the path with their fingers.  

They note it is wise to race twice.  
The sun is up, and the day is hot.  

“Let’s grab some ice to cool,” says Meg.  
They sip on the ice and plan.  

Meg spots a group of mice in the grass.  
“Look at them race!” Ben laughs.  

“Those mice are quick!” says Meg. 
Now it is time to race at last. 
 
Ben and Meg start the race.  
They run with a smile across the green space.

question: Where will the race end?
ground_truth_answer: The big tree

question: What is Herb's job on the farm?
ground_truth_answer: farmer
document:
Mister Herb is a farmer.  
He has a sister named Fern.  
Herb and Fern have a big farm.  

There is a herd of cows on the farm.  
One day, Fern wrote a letter.  
Her letter had a rhyme and a verse.  

Fern asked Herb to read it.  
“Read it in a funny voice,” said Fern.  
Herb found it hard at first.  

He had to observe each word.  
Fern sat on her perch and laughed.  
Her laugh made Herb smile.  

After some tries, he did better.  
Reading aloud had a perk.  
It made Herb feel super.
  
“I can serve more and more people!” said Herb.  
More kids came from far and wide.  
They loved to hear Herb read.  

A clerk from town asked for more fun.  
The term for fun was to start.  
Fern and Herb worked together.  

Their rhymes made each day bright.  
The farm became a happy place.  
People loved the fun and words.

# 1st Grade Text
## Min, Kim, and the Big Dig
Min has a kit.
Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

question: What was Ming doing when she found the ring?
ground_truth_answer: digging in the sand
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## The Missing Ring
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

The hogs do not stop!

Jobs on a farm do not stop.

question: What was the family doing when they got to the hill?
document:
Luis and Maya were going camping with Dad. They were packing a tent, a bag, and food. Maya was singing as she put on a red cap. Luis was humming as he looked at a map.
When they got to the hill, Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What did Stan lose?
ground_truth_answer: His red stamp.
document:
Stan had a red stamp on his desk.
He did not use the stamp a lot, but he liked it.
One day, Stan went to get the stamp.
The stamp was not on the desk!
Stan ran to Star.
“Star, I lost my red stamp. Can you help me?”
“Yes,” said Star. “Let us stop and think. Did you set the stamp on a stand?”
Stan did stop and think.
“I did set the stamp on the big stand in class,” he said.
Star and Stan went to the stand.
The stamp was not there.
Next, Star and Stan went to the steps.
“Did the stamp fall on the steps?” Star said.
Stan bent to look.
There was no stamp on the steps.
At last, Stan went to the back desk.
The stamp was stuck to a stack of pads!
Stan held up the stamp.
“Here it is!” he said.
Star and Stan clap.
“Next time, I will not lose my stamp,” Stan said.
Star and Stan went back to class with a big grin.

question: What can fish do in a pond?
document:
Fish can swim fast in a pond. A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash. Some fish can rush to get food. A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.
Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

question: Why was finding the ring the best part of Ming's day?
document:
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

question: Who went camping with Dad?
ground_truth_answer: Luis and Maya went camping with Dad.
document:
# 2nd Grade Text
# The Camping Trip
Luis and Maya were going camping with Dad. They were packing a tent, a bag, and food. Maya was singing as she put on a red cap. Luis was humming as he looked at a map.
When they got to the hill, Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What were Luis and Maya doing before they started camping?
ground_truth_answer: Maya was singing as she put on a red cap, and Luis was looking at a map.

question: What does Min have?
ground_truth_answer: Min has a kit.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## Min, Kim, and the Big Dig
Min has a kit.
Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

question: What does Kim have?
ground_truth_answer: Kim has a lid.

Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

question: Have you ever helped someone with a task? Tell about a time when you worked with a friend to accomplish something.
ground_truth_answer: Yes, I once helped my friend build a sandcastle at the beach and we both felt proud of our creation.

question: What does a fish use to swim fast in a pond?
ground_truth_answer: A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## Fish in the Pond
Fish can swim fast in a pond. A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash. Some fish can rush to get food. A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.
Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

question: Where was Ming digging when she found the ring?
ground_truth_answer: In the sand.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## The Missing Ring
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

question: What was the ring doing that helped Ming see it?
ground_truth_answer: Shining in the sun.

ground_truth_answer: Sam has a red hat.

Mom and Dad have a big map.  

They plan to go to the park. 
 
Sam has a red hat and a blue bag.  

At the park, Sam sees a flag.  

He runs fast to grab it.  

Mom calls, "Can we sit at the mat?"  

Dad gets a pan with ham and jam.  

Sam is glad to eat ham and jam.  

They all sit and rest by the lamp.  

At last, they look at the map and plan to go home.

question: What did Chuck and Chip do at the ranch?
ground_truth_answer: They chopped a big log with an ax.
document:
Once upon a time, at a sunny ranch, there was a chap named Chuck.  

Chuck had a job to chop the wood for lunch.  

His chum, Chip, came to help.  

They sat on the bench to chat and made a plan.  

First, they went to check the big log.  

They used an ax to chop inch by inch.  

Each chop made a loud crunch. 
 
A small chick ran to check the noise. 
 
"It is much work," said Chuck, wiping his chin.  

Chip had a hunch they needed more help.  

They pulled a bunch of logs to the side.  

As they worked, they took a sip to quench thirst.  

At last, they clinched the task.  

They were glad to have such a good friend.

question: Where did the loud crunch sound come from?
ground_truth_answer: It came from the ax chopping the log.

Each chop made a loud crunch. 
 
A small chick ran to check the noise. 
 
"It is much work," said Chuck, wiping his chin.  

Chip had a hunch they needed more help.  

They pulled a bunch of logs to the side.  

As they worked, they took a sip to quench thirst.  

At last, they clinched the task.  

They were glad to have such a good friend.

question: How did the work help their friendship?

Once upon a time, at a sunny ranch, there was a chap named Chuck.  

Chuck had a job to chop the wood for lunch.  

His chum, Chip, came to help.  

They sat on the bench to chat and made a plan.  

First, they went to check the big log.  

They used an ax to chop inch by inch.  

Each chop made a loud crunch. 
 
A small chick ran to check the noise. 
 
"It is much work," said Chuck, wiping his chin.  

Chip had a hunch they needed more help.  

They pulled a bunch of logs to the side.  

As they worked, they took a sip to quench thirst.  

At last, they clinched the task.  

They were glad to have such a good friend.

question: Where does the race start?
ground_truth_answer: The race starts at the red gate.
document:
Ben and Meg are at the park.  
They have a race to plan.  

“This race will be nice!” says Meg.  
Ben grins and sets a fast pace.
  
The race will start at the red gate.  
The first one to the big tree will win. 
 
They hope to win a fun prize.  
Ben and Meg trace the path with their fingers.  

They note it is wise to race twice.  
The sun is up, and the day is hot.  

“Let’s grab some ice to cool,” says Meg.  
They sip on the ice and plan.  

Meg spots a group of mice in the grass.  
“Look at them race!” Ben laughs.  

“Those mice are quick!” says Meg. 
Now it is time to race at last. 
 
Ben and Meg start the race.  
They run with a smile across the green space.

question: What is Herb’s job on the farm?
ground_truth_answer: Herb is a farmer.
document:
Mister Herb is a farmer.  
He has a sister named Fern.  
Herb and Fern have a big farm.  

There is a herd of cows on the farm.  
One day, Fern wrote a letter.  
Her letter had a rhyme and a verse.  

Fern asked Herb to read it.  
“Read it in a funny voice,” said Fern.  
Herb found it hard at first.  

He had to observe each word.  
Fern sat on her perch and laughed.  
Her laugh made Herb smile.  

After some tries, he did better.  
Reading aloud had a perk.  
It made Herb feel super.
  
“I can serve more and more people!” said Herb.  
More kids came from far and wide.  
They loved to hear Herb read.  

A clerk from town asked for more fun.  
The term for fun was to start.  
Fern and Herb worked together.  

Their rhymes made each day bright.  
The farm became a happy place.  
People loved the fun and words.

question: What did Tom want to build?
ground_truth_answer: Tom wanted to build a cool treehouse.
document:
Tom was a small boy with a big dream.  
He wanted to build a cool treehouse.  
On Tuesday, he got a blueprint for his plan.  
The blueprint was new and very blue.  

His mom came to see his plan.  
She looked at it and smiled.  
Tom and his mom began to gather items.  
They found wood, nails, and a hammer. 
 
Tom's sister, Sue, also came to help.  
She brought some juice and fruit.  
Tom asked, "Can I have a sip?"  
Sue gave him juice and a big red apple. 
 
The wind blew strong all day.  
But they did not stop.  
Tom and Sue took turns with the hammer.  
Their arms got tired, but they kept on. 
 
At last, they had built the base.  
They were glad and took a break.  
Tom drew a big sun on the blueprint.  
He said, "This will be the top of the treehouse."  

On the next day, the work began again.  
Tom felt like it was a long cruise, not work.  
He ate some fruit while thinking.  
Sue said, "I will chew some gum."  

Tom laughed and nodded.  
After much effort, the treehouse was done.  
It was a fine new place for fun.  
Tom and Sue celebrated with more juice.

question: What did Max and Anna pack for their adventure?
ground_truth_answer: A map and snacks.
document:
Max and Anna have a plan.  
They want to go on an adventure in nature.  
They get up early.  
Max and Anna pack a map and snacks.  
The weather is nice.  

Max takes a picture of the morning sky.  
They start down the path near their house.  
This adventure is to capture fun.  
Anna spots a bird.  
She points to it.  

Max nods and follows her.  
They walk into the forest.  
The path is filled with small stones.  
The path leads them to a sunny spot.  
Anna says the flowers have a soft texture.  
Max takes more pictures of the flowers.  

They see a mix of colors.  
Anna picks one flower.  
She gives it to Max.  
They follow the path and find a stream.  
Max listens to the sound of water flowing.  

They sit by the stream.  
Max and Anna enjoy their snacks there.  
Anna writes her name on a rock as a signature.  
Max adds his name too.  
Every step is a part of the adventure.  
Soon, the sun sets over the meadow.  

It is time for their departure.  
Max leads the way back home.  
Anna talks about all the fun they had.  
At home, they look at the pictures.  
The pictures are full of joy. 
 
Anna smiles and says, “Let’s do it again!”  
Max nods, feeling happy and full.  
Their adventure was a grand mixture of fun.  
Every picture tells a story of the day.  
They will nurture their friendship and adventures.  
The day ends with memories made.

document:
# 1st Grade Text
## Min, Kim, and the Big Dig
Min has a kit.
Kim has a lid.
They dig in the big pit.
“Dig, Min! Dig, Kim!”
A bug is in the pit.
The bug is big and red.
Min can lift the lid.
Kim can pin the bug in the kit.
The bug will not slip.
“Look, Kim! We did it!” said Min.
Kim had a big grin.
They put the bug in the kit.
Min, Kim, and the bug sit in the sun.
They had fun and did a big job.

question: Where did Ming find the ring?
ground_truth_answer: Ming found the ring in the sand.
document:
# 1st Grade Text
## The Missing Ring
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.

question: Why was the ring shining?
ground_truth_answer: The ring was shining in the sun.

The hogs trot to the food.
The hogs do not stop!
Sol can jog to the pond.
She can hop on a rock.
She can spot a frog or a log.
Jobs on a farm do not stop.
A farm has lots to do!

question: What were Luis and Maya doing when they got to the hill?
ground_truth_answer: Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
document:
Luis and Maya were going camping with Dad. They were packing a tent, a bag, and food. Maya was singing as she put on a red cap. Luis was humming as he looked at a map.
When they got to the hill, Dad was lifting the big tent. Luis was helping by holding the tent up. Maya was running to get sticks for the fire.
As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

As night came, the kids were sitting by the fire. They were roasting nuts and sipping hot milk. The fire was crackling and popping. The kids were singing a silly song.
Then Maya heard a rustling in the grass. “What is running in the dark?” she said. Luis was grabbing a lamp. The lamp was shining on a small dog!
The dog was wagging and licking. It was sniffing at the food and jumping at the bag. The kids were giggling. Dad was smiling.
The dog kept resting by the fire. It was curling up and napping. Luis and Maya kept singing. Dad was humming. The fire was glowing, and the stars were twinkling.
It was the best night of camping.

question: What did Stan lose?
ground_truth_answer: Stan lost his red stamp.
document:
Stan had a red stamp on his desk.
He did not use the stamp a lot, but he liked it.
One day, Stan went to get the stamp.
The stamp was not on the desk!
Stan ran to Star.
“Star, I lost my red stamp. Can you help me?”
“Yes,” said Star. “Let us stop and think. Did you set the stamp on a stand?”
Stan did stop and think.
“I did set the stamp on the big stand in class,” he said.
Star and Stan went to the stand.
The stamp was not there.
Next, Star and Stan went to the steps.
“Did the stamp fall on the steps?” Star said.
Stan bent to look.
There was no stamp on the steps.
At last, Stan went to the back desk.
The stamp was stuck to a stack of pads!
Stan held up the stamp.
“Here it is!” he said.
Star and Stan clap.
“Next time, I will not lose my stamp,” Stan said.
Star and Stan went back to class with a big grin.

question: What do fish do when they want to move fast?
ground_truth_answer: Fish swish their fins to dash and splash.
document:
Fish can swim fast in a pond. A fish will swish its fins to dash and splash. Some fish can rush to get food. A fish can snap up a bug or a small bit of mash.
Fish can hide in mud or in a big bush by the pond. This helps the fish when a crab or a bird rushes in.
A fish has a soft body and the skin has fins. The fins can flash in the sun.
Some fish live in fresh ponds. Some fish live in the big sea. Fish can be big or small. Fish can be red or tan. All fish can swim and splash!

question: Where was Ming digging?
ground_truth_answer: Ming was digging in the sand.
document:
Ming was digging in the sand. She was hoping to find a shell.
As she was digging, she saw a ring. The ring was shining in the sun.
Ming went running to Dad.
“Look! I found a ring!” she said.
Dad was smiling. “That is Mom’s ring! She was missing it.”
Mom came rushing from the deck.
“My ring! Thank you for bringing it to me, Ming!”
Mom gave Ming a big hug.
Ming kept smiling. Finding the ring was the best part of the day.
